title: eucalyptus kochii subsp. borealis
intro: Eucalyptus kochii subsp. borealis, also known as the Northern Eucalyptus, is a subspecies of the Eucalyptus kochii tree native to the mountains of Central America. The leaves of this plant contain compounds such as eucalyptol, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Eucalyptus kochii subsp. borealis have been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
